Our Story, Our Mission

About Us

Driven by lived experience, united by purpose — empowering refugees and host communities to build dignified, sustainable futures.

Since 2023

Serving Communities

Who We Are

Mwinja Divine Help Foundation

We are a Refugee-Led Organization founded in Kampala, Uganda, born from the lived experiences of those who have directly faced the challenges of displacement.

Our founders understand intimately the struggles faced by refugees and host communities — the daily fight for education, healthcare, employment, and dignity. This understanding drives everything we do.

We are committed to creating sustainable pathways out of poverty through education, economic empowerment, and community development — ensuring that every individual has the opportunity to thrive.

5,000+

Beneficiaries

12

Projects

25+

Communities

Our Journey

Our Story

2023

The Beginning

Mwinja Divine Help Foundation was founded in Kampala, Uganda, by refugees who understood firsthand the challenges faced by displaced communities.

2024

First Programs

We launched our first education and livelihood programs, reaching over 500 beneficiaries in Rubaga Division and surrounding communities.

2025

Growth & Recognition

Registered with Uganda NGO Bureau, expanded our team, and established partnerships with local organizations and international agencies.

2026

Scaling Impact

Today we serve thousands of refugees and host community members, with programs spanning education, health, economic empowerment, and community development.

What Drives Us

Mission, Vision & Values

Compassion

Treating every individual with kindness, empathy, and understanding

Empowerment

Enabling individuals to take control of their own futures

Inclusivity

Creating welcoming spaces for all, regardless of background

Sustainability

Focusing on long-term solutions that benefit future generations

Our Vision

A world where refugees and low-income host communities are socially and economically empowered, actively participating in community development and living with dignity.

Our Mission

To promote psychosocial well-being, provide education access, create economic opportunities, and foster community cohesion for vulnerable refugees and host communities in Uganda.

Our Leadership

Meet Our Team

Dedicated professionals with shared lived experience and commitment to serving our communities.

Miriam Akello

Executive Director

Founded MDHF after her own refugee journey, dedicated to empowering displaced communities.

James Opondo

Program Manager

15+ years experience in community development and humanitarian work.

Sarah Nakato

Education Lead

Passionate about education access for refugee children and youth.

David Okello

Livelihoods Coordinator

Expert in vocational training and small business development.

Why We Exist

A Crisis Demands
a Response

Uganda hosts Africa's largest refugee population — over 1.96 million people forced to flee their homes due to conflict, violence, and persecution.

In Kampala alone, over 157,000 refugees face daily challenges: limited access to education, scarce livelihood opportunities, inadequate healthcare, and the constant struggle for dignity and self-reliance.

We exist because no one should have to choose between safety and opportunity. We exist because refugees deserve a chance to not just survive — but to thrive.

Join Our Mission

Whether you have resources, skills, or simply the desire to make a difference, there is a place for you in our work. Together, we can build futures.

“To protect and love, care and support vulnerable people”
